adapterId.description:
Optional adapter identifier to bind the capability to. When present, any
consuming registration or launch-plan preflight must use the same
manifest adapter_id.

oneOf.description:
Optional requested browser suppression posture to bind into a later
launch-plan preflight. When present, the nested admission request must
carry the same sensitive_activity_mode.

ttlSeconds.description:
Optional requested lifetime in seconds. When present, it must be between
1 and 300 seconds.

sameUserCapability.description:
Secret one-time capability. Treat as bearer material; Cradle stores only
a digest and never echoes it from consuming preflight responses.

evidenceField.description: Required evidence field path in BrowserAdapterCompletionReport.

label.description: Human-readable compatibility label retained for older manifest fields.

proofId.description: Stable machine-readable proof name. Adapters must echo this in reports.

requiredInvariant.description: Runtime invariant Cradle expects before trusting completion.

adapterId.description: Must match the trusted adapter id chosen for launch.

completionBoundToClaimedLaunch.description:
True only for shape-complete reports that match a claimed server-issued
launch envelope template. This is still not production teardown
verification.

completionReportTemplateMatched.description:
True when the submitted report exactly matches the completion report
template embedded in the recorded launch envelope.

launchRequestClaimed.description:
True when the server-issued launch request was already claimed by an
adapter through the launch-claim preflight.

launchRequestEnvelopeMatched.description:
True when request_id, adapter_id, and contract_version match the
canonical launch envelope recorded by this daemon.

serverIssuedLaunchRequest.description:
True when the report request_id is still present in this daemon's
bounded launch replay ledger.

launchRequest.description:
Full server-issued launch request returned by
/v1/browser/adapter/launch/plan. Cradle compares it against the stored
canonical envelope before marking the request id claimed.

sameUserCapability.description:
One-time same-user capability issued by the REST control plane. It is
consumed on a matching preflight and never echoed.

adapterContractFieldsComplete.description:
True when the submitted adapter manifest satisfies the published field
contract before endpoint network-policy binding. This is not
launchability.

replayProtectionBound.description:
True when this daemon recorded the emitted launch request id in its
bounded replay ledger. This is not launch authorization.

requestAdapterCompatibility.description:
Request-scoped adapter compatibility for this admission. This can be
true even when global adapter conformance is false because the manifest
does not claim every planned browser level.

adapterId.description:
Adapter identifier for the envelope. Runtime launch requests use the
trusted registered adapter id; conformance fixtures may carry a sample
id and still do not imply adapter registration, trust, or launchability.
Null in discovery templates before an adapter is selected.

expiresAt.description:
RFC3339 timestamp after which a future launcher must reject this
envelope instead of attempting adapter execution.

issuedAt.description:
RFC3339 timestamp for when this envelope was issued. Discovery and
conformance templates use a deterministic placeholder; live launch-plan
preflights use server time.

maxSessionSeconds.description: Maximum browser session lifetime represented by this envelope.

replayProtectionRequired.description: Future launchers must reject reused request_id values for this adapter.

requestId.description: Server-issued request identifier for adapter logs and completion proofs.

requestedControls.description:
Effective controls the future adapter must satisfy for this admission:
requested profile controls, caller-required controls, and
sensitive_activity_mode controls.

adapterId.description: Stable adapter identifier; non-empty, at most 128 bytes, no surrounding whitespace.

completionProofs.description: Required completion proof labels; entries must be non-empty without surrounding whitespace.

contractVersion.description: Browser adapter contract version; non-empty with no surrounding whitespace.

guardFields.description: Required guard_plan field names; entries must be non-empty without surrounding whitespace.

launchEndpoint.description: Optional HTTPS launch endpoint string. Only syntax and literal local/private hosts are checked.

sameUserCapability.description:
Caller-supplied same-user capability for the future local user/session
that would own this adapter. Cradle can bind only a live one-time
capability issued by its REST control plane, consumes it at most once,
and never echoes it in responses.

compatible.description:
True when the submitted adapter manifest satisfies the requested
admission level, required controls, guard fields, and completion proofs.
This is request-scoped compatibility, not global conformance or
launchability.

artifactMode.description:
Artifact persistence posture requested for the session. Non-default
modes remain fail-closed until storage and sealing are implemented.

credentialMode.description:
Credential posture requested for the session. Non-default modes remain
fail-closed until a real browser substrate implements scoped handling.

sensitiveActivityMode.description:
Requested privacy/suppression posture for sensitive browser work. This
is part of the future adapter contract and is still fail-closed until a
production browser launcher enforces it.

targetOrigins.description:
Bare HTTP(S) origins this browser session is allowed to target. Entries
must contain only scheme, host, and optional port. Admission rejects
credentials, paths, queries, fragments, localhost names, literal
private/LAN IP ranges, literal link-local metadata targets, and more
than 16 entries. Hostname targets are syntax-validated only here and
must be revalidated after DNS, proxying, redirects, retries, and final
socket selection before any future runnable session connects.

adapterHandoff.description:
Adapter handoff metadata for future browser launchers. This is
fail-closed while launch_endpoint is null and launchable is false.

artifactMode.description: Echo of the requested artifact persistence posture.

credentialMode.description: Echo of the requested credential posture.

guardPlan.description:
Structured guard posture a future browser adapter must enforce before
this admission request can become runnable. It is a plan, not a claim
that the current daemon enforces browser isolation.

intentWarnings.description:
Non-fatal intent issues that matter before any future runnable browser
session can be admitted.

sensitiveActivityMode.description: Echo of the requested privacy/suppression posture.

sensitiveActivityModeCompatible.description:
True when the requested sensitive_activity_mode can be satisfied by the
requested browser sandbox level once that level is implemented.

sensitiveActivityModeCompatibleLevels.description:
Browser levels published as compatible with the requested
sensitive_activity_mode in /v1/browser/profiles.

sensitiveActivityModeMissingControls.description:
Required controls for sensitive_activity_mode that the requested browser
level does not provide.

sensitiveActivityModeRequiredControls.description:
Controls the requested sensitive_activity_mode requires from the selected
browser profile before the mode can ever become runnable.

targetOrigins.description: Echo of the validated target origins from the admission request.

allowedOrigins.description:
Syntax-validated origins this browser session may target. Hostname
origins still require runtime DNS/proxy/redirect/final-socket policy
binding before a session can become runnable.

denyLocalhost.description:
Future runtime/adapter must reject localhost targets before any session
becomes runnable.

denyMetadataEndpoints.description:
Future runtime/adapter must reject cloud metadata endpoints before any
session becomes runnable.

denyPrivateNetworks.description:
Future runtime/adapter must deny localhost, loopback, private, shared,
link-local, and metadata-address egress even after DNS resolution,
redirects, and proxying before any session becomes runnable.

outboundNetworkDisabledWithoutProxy.description:
Future runtime/adapter must disable direct outbound networking when a
required proxy is unavailable before any session becomes runnable.

requireDnsRebindingProtection.description:
Future runtime/adapter must protect against DNS rebinding before any
session becomes runnable.

requireProxyEnforcement.description:
Future runtime/adapter must enforce outbound proxy policy before any
session becomes runnable.

requireRedirectRevalidation.description:
Future runtime/adapter must revalidate redirects before any session
becomes runnable.

aetherPayment.description:
Aether-compatible payment evidence accepted by the MCP boundary. The
payload header is never echoed; only its companion hash may be surfaced
in MCP response metadata for downstream audit correlation.

CreateModuleRequest.description: Upload body for a reusable, content-addressed WebAssembly module.

EgressChannel.description:
Named egress channels a caller can request under `policy.egress`. Each channel
is a managed, credential-attaching forwarding endpoint the daemon provides to
executed code; raw network access is never granted. Unknown channel names are
rejected by deserialization (like every other policy key), so a typo cannot
silently widen exposure.

`tempera-llm` grants the executed code a single forwarding endpoint (exposed
as `TEMPERA_LLM_PROXY_URL`) that relays only to the daemon-configured
tempera-llm gateway, with the daemon attaching its held bearer token
server-side so user code never sees credentials.

EgressUsage.description:
Managed forwarding-endpoint usage for one egress channel, recorded in the
execution result so an auditor can see how much a run relied on external
services. `bytes_out` is the total request payload the executed code sent into
the forwarding endpoint; `bytes_in` is the total response payload relayed back.

egressUsage.description:
Per-channel managed-egress usage counters for auditability. Empty unless a
`policy.egress` channel was granted and the executed code used it. Because
a granted egress channel reaches a non-deterministic external service, any
run with a non-empty list here also reports `deterministic: false`.

ExecutionStatus.description: Execution outcome. `timeout` means a fuel or wall-time budget was exhausted; `killed` means the caller or host explicitly canceled execution.

cpuTimeMs.description:
CPU time in milliseconds, when the lane measures it separately from wall
time. The W0 wasm lane does not, so this is `None` there — use `fuel_used`
as the deterministic compute signal rather than treating wall time as CPU.

Module.description: Metadata for a durable, content-addressed WebAssembly module.

sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 digest of the decoded WebAssembly bytes.

egress.description:
Managed egress channels granted to the executed code. Empty (the default)
means the sandbox has no reachable network at all. Each named channel is a
daemon-provided, credential-attaching forwarding endpoint — never raw
network access. A lane must reject a channel it cannot enforce, and the
daemon must fail closed when a requested channel is not configured.

info.description: HTTP and MCP server surfaces for cradle.
info.title: cradle API

200.description: Issue a short-lived one-time browser adapter same-user capability

401.description: Missing auth or daemon auth is not configured

200.description: Fail-closed browser adapter completion report validation
Fail-closed browser adapter contract and conformance profile discovery
REST-only browser adapter launch request replay claim
Fail-closed browser adapter launch plan preflight

401.description: Missing auth or daemon auth is not configured

400.description: Malformed, oversized, or non-JSON request body

422.description: Protocol, source, policy, or sync-limit rejection

parameters.description: Optional deduplication key; this header overrides request.idempotency_key
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

409.description: Idempotency key reused with a different payload

422.description: Protocol, source, policy, or job-limit rejection

429.description: Worker, SQLite admission, or retained-state capacity exhausted

409.description: Job already finished and cannot be canceled

parameters.description: Project id bound to the introspected central token
parameters.name: project

400.description: Invalid or oversized base64 WebAssembly binary

401.description: Missing or invalid central control-plane bearer

404.description: Project is not the token-bound project

parameters.description: Project id bound to the introspected central token
parameters.name: project

401.description: Missing or invalid central control-plane bearer
